channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Foster Home Shortage in British Columbia Raises Concerns for Vulnerable Children’s Care
item.description: British Columbia is facing a growing shortage of foster homes, leading to significant challenges in the province’s child welfare system. Social service workers and child advocates warn that the lack o...

item.title: Tanzanian Peace Supports Creativity and Growth in Fashion and Entrepreneurship
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, fashion designer Agusta Masaki of AM Fashion highlighted that the peace enjoyed in Tanzania plays a vital role in fostering creativity and business development. Unlike other countrie...

item.title: Punjab Assembly Faces Backlash Over Mining Bills Allowing Operations in Protected Forests
item.description: The Punjab government has come under severe criticism after the Standing Committee on Forests and Wildlife of the Punjab Assembly approved three amendment bills that would permit mining in protected a...

item.title: Scientists Explore Nuclear Clocks for Unprecedented Timekeeping Accuracy
item.description: Most clocks today, from wristwatches to GPS systems, rely on regular, repeating motions to measure time accurately. Atomic clocks improve on this by using electron transitions in atoms, offering extra...

item.title: Ontario explores grading changes amid declining high school attendance
item.description: Attendance at Ontario high schools has fallen significantly, with only 40% of students in grades 9 to 12 meeting the province's attendance threshold of 90% of classes in the 2024-25 school year. The d...

item.title: Bombay HC Rejects Abu Salem's Immediate Release Plea
item.description: The Bombay High Court has given a thumbs down to gangster Abu Salem Abdul Qayoom Ansari’s request to be released from prison right away. Ansari, who’s been facing charges related to the 1993 Mumbai bl...

item.title: UBC scientists develop eco-friendly fruit wash to extend freshness of produce
item.description: Researchers at the University of British Columbia (UBC) have developed a new biodegradable fruit and vegetable wash designed to keep fresh produce safe and edible for longer periods. This innovative p...

item.title: BBC exposes fake LGBT asylum claims facilitated by unscrupulous legal advisers
item.description: A BBC undercover investigation has uncovered a network of unregulated legal advisers and law firms in the UK that help migrants fabricate stories about being gay to obtain asylum. The probe revealed t...

item.title: New Fountain Gate Coach Minziro Prepares for Tough League Debut Against Simba SC
item.description: Fred Felix “Minziro”, newly appointed as Fountain Gate FC head coach, will face a stern test in his first match in charge as his side takes on Simba SC in the Tanzania Mainland Premier League at Sheik...

item.title: Kelowna Woman Saved by Liver Transplant from Living Donor
item.description: A Kelowna, British Columbia woman, Lyndsay Richholt, has been given a second chance at life after receiving a liver transplant from a living donor, Robyn Ralph, an Alberta woman she had never met befo...

item.title: Exploring Planetary Caves to Search for Life and Human Habitats Beyond Earth
item.description: Subterranean environments on Earth provide a model for how life might exist elsewhere in the solar system. Scientists like Penelope Boston, a NASA portfolio scientist, have studied Earth's caves for d...

item.title: Pakistan Advocates for Reform of UNSC Veto Power, Rejects Expansion of Permanent Membership
item.description: Pakistan has reiterated its long-standing position at the United Nations, urging either the abolition or significant restriction of veto power held by permanent members of the UN Security Council (UNS...

item.title: Investigation reveals advisers helping migrants falsify asylum claims based on sexuality
item.description: A BBC investigation has uncovered that certain immigration advisers are assisting migrants in the UK to make fraudulent asylum claims by posing as gay. The investigation involved a reporter posing as ...

item.title: Trump likens himself to Jesus, raising questions about political symbolism
item.description: The article discusses Donald Trump's recent public self-comparison to Jesus Christ, framing it as a dramatic and historically reminiscent moment akin to the excesses of Roman emperors like Caligula. T...

item.title: Court Ruling May Cost Gina Rinehart Hundreds of Millions
item.description: Australian mining magnate Gina Rinehart, one of the nation’s wealthiest individuals, faces a potential financial loss amounting to hundreds of millions of dollars after suffering a major defeat in cou...

item.title: Many Manila Jeepney Drivers Yet to Receive Fuel Subsidy
item.description: As of April 15, 2026, hundreds of public utility jeepney (PUJ) drivers in Manila remain excluded from the government's fuel subsidy program. The subsidy, intended to ease the rising cost of fuel for t...

item.title: Investigation into TCS Nashik Case Involving Alleged Harassment and Financial Exploitation of Recruits
item.description: The TCS Nashik incident reportedly involves allegations of sexual harassment and unethical recruitment practices affecting young recruits in need of money. According to sources, certain individuals al...

item.title: Iran Conflict Causes Fertiliser Shortages Threatening Food Security in Developing Countries
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran dey cause wahala for fertiliser supply, wey fit shake food security for plenty developing countries. Pamela Coke-Hamilton, wey be executive director for International Trade Cen...

item.title: Impact of the Iran War on UK Economic and Defence Policy
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran is intensifying the challenges faced by the UK government, particularly regarding economic stability and defence spending. According to the International Monetary Fund, the eco...

item.title: Zak Butters fined for alleged umpire abuse amid family frustration
item.description: Port Adelaide midfielder Zak Butters has been fined $1500 following allegations of abusive language directed at umpire Nick Foot during a recent match against St Kilda. The AFL tribunal concluded that...
